conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Next question: Are you the person responsible for Alex? By this I mean, are you the main person who looks after Alex and is responsible for their care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. Alex lives with me and I look after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask: Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? This would typically mean they're in school or college, but not university-level education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is only 8 years old, so they're just in primary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that's helpful to know.

One more important question: Do you or your partner (if you have one) have an individual income over £50,000 per year? This is important because it may affect whether a tax charge applies to your Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of us has an income over £50,000 per year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information.

Based on what you've told me, let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex:

- You have a child (Alex) who is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's care and upbringing
- Alex is under 16 years old (and in primary school)
- Neither you nor your partner has an income over £50,000

This means you meet the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements because:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex and they live with you
- Neither you nor your partner earns over £50,000, so you won't face any tax charges

You can apply for Child Benefit and should receive payments for Alex. If you haven't already applied, you can do so through the government's official channels.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered and the council pays for their accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under the age limit of 16 (or under 20 if in approved education or training). The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, who lives with them. Alex attends primary school. Neither the claimant nor their partner has an income over £50,000, so no High Income Child Benefit Charge would apply. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them in the UK, and there are no income-related charges to consider as neither parent earns over £50,000 per year.

The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, who lives with them. Alex attends primary school. Neither the claimant nor their partner has an income over £50,000, so no High Income Child Benefit Charge would apply. All eligibility criteria are met.

They meet all the key requirements: they are responsible for a child under 16 who lives with them in the UK, and there are no income-related charges to consider as neither parent earns over £50,000 per year.
